title: The Economics of Random Investments in Financial Markets
abstract: This paper investigates the role of randomness in financial markets at both micro and macro levels. Using statistical physics techniques, we compare the effectiveness of random investment strategies with predictive strategies. Our agent-based models show that random strategies can help reduce financial bubbles and crashes through self-organized criticality.

analysis.summary: This article explores the counterintuitive idea that randomness can play a constructive role in financial markets. The authors use econophysics methods to show that random investment strategies can be as effective as analytical ones.
analysis.raw_summary: A well-structured interdisciplinary paper on the role of randomness in economics, combining physics and finance perspectives.
